titleStmt.title: SII 41.25: original edition by S. Rajagopal
No. 25. (A.R. No. 24 of 1905.) Kīḻūr, Tirukkoyilur Taluk, South Arcot District.

summary.p: Slab detached from the temple and placed near the gōpura in the Vīraṭṭāṉēśvara temple.

This records the gift of 40 kaḻañju of gold by the Ūrkkaṟ-semmai, left in the hands of the members of the Śabhai of Attaḷam-poḷḷil-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a division of Kuṟukkai-kūṟṟam. The members of the Śabha agreed to measure out 40 kalam of paddy (at the rate of 1 kalam per kaḻañju) to the treasury (bhaṇḍāra) of Tiruvīraṭṭānattāḷvār temple at Tirukkōvalūr, in Kuṟukkai-kūṟṟam, a district of Malāḍu. The purpose of the gift is not clear.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . kannaradevaṟku yāṇṭu patiṉe
ṭṭāvatu malāṭṭuk kūṟukkaik kūṟṟattu tirukkovalu
r tiruviraṭṭāṉaThe letters in brackets are conjecturally restored, since the stone has been bored at those points.ttāḷvā[r paThe letters in brackets are conjecturally restored, since the stone has been bored at those points.] ṇṭārattu Ikkūṟṟattu
Attaḷa[ mpoḷḷiThe letters in brackets are conjecturally restored, since the stone has been bored at those points.] catu[ rveti*] maṅkalat[ tu saThe letters in brackets are conjecturally restored, since the stone has been bored at those points.] bhaiyo
m koṇṭapoṉ Ūrkkaṟ cemmai nāṟpatiṉ kaḻañcum I
p poṉṉukku kaḻañciṉvāy kalap poliyūṭṭāka nā
(va)ṟpatiṉ kala nelp peṟiḷamaiyāka kūlipparam paṭ
ṭut tiru[ muṟṟatThe letters in brackets are conjecturally restored, since the stone has been bored at those points.] tile Āṭṭāṇṭu toṟu[ m cantrāThe letters in brackets are conjecturally restored, since the stone has been bored at those points. ndrā] din=taval
tūyavākki Aḷakka kaṭappomāṉom Innellu
ntaṇṭa vantāṟkku meykkaṇṭu coṟu kuṭuppo
māṉom Attaḷampoḷḷil sabhaiyom Itu
panmāheśvara rakṣai .
Digital edition of SII 41.25 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
